title: GitHub: Creating Repos, Pushing Code, and README Writing
content:
### Day 39: GitHub: Creating Repos, Pushing Code, and README Writing

**GitHub** hosts your code and serves as your portfolio. Hiring managers check your GitHub profile.

**Key Actions:** Create repo, push code, write README, pin best projects.

**Good README:** Title, description, setup instructions, screenshots, tech stack.
codeExample:
# Day 39: GitHub: Creating Repos, Pushing Code, and README Writing
# GitHub Workflow (terminal commands)

# Connect to GitHub
# git remote add origin https://github.com/you/project.git
# git branch -M main
# git push -u origin main

# README template
readme = """# Project Name

Brief description of what this project does.

## Setup
\x60\x60\x60bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python main.py
\x60\x60\x60

## Features
- Feature 1
- Feature 2

## Tech Stack
- Python 3.10
- scikit-learn
- FastAPI

## Results
| Model | Accuracy | F1 |
|-------|----------|----|
| RF    | 0.87     | 0.83 |
| XGB   | 0.91     | 0.88 |
"""
print(readme)
practiceQuestions: How to push code to GitHub?
How to write a project description?
keyTakeaways: GitHub hosts your code and serves as your portfolio.
Hiring managers check your GitHub profile.
Key Actions: Create repo, push code, write README, pin best projects.
